rows.description: Recepta pro hoc Anno, ad 2dum diem Novembris. A.D. 1819.
rows.notes: Top title line is fully legible in clear ink; no monetary columns associated with this line.

Description partially archaic but readable as 'Arrearagia Recepta'. Numeric columns align clearly with vertical rules. Pence has a small loop indicating a quarter (1/4) — read as 0.25. Slight uncertainty in exact spelling of the Latinized description.
Description and amounts are clear. Columns align perfectly; values (30/0/0) are unambiguous.
Text and numbers are clear. Pence shows a single '1' with no fraction; column alignment unambiguous.

Subtotal row (no left-hand description printed) shows numbers aligned under the three currency columns. Sum matches arithmetic of previous three rows (970.16.2¼ + 30.0.0 + 1754.0.1 = 2754.16.3¼). Column alignment clear.
Description legible. Numeric columns align with previous totals. Pence reads as '0' with a small fractional mark interpreted as 1/4 (0.25); placement consistent with pence column.
Label and numbers are clear and well aligned. Arithmetic check: 2754.16.3¼ + 1647.9.0¼ = 4402.5.3½; pence fraction clearly half (0.5).
Section heading is centered/indented and contains no amounts; text is legible.

Description is in abbreviated Latin; letters are generally legible though some punctuation/abbreviations ambiguous. Numeric columns align; amounts read as 700 pounds, 11 shillings, 7 pence with no fraction evident.
Description clear. Pence shows '11' with a half-penny loop (ob), interpreted as 11½ (0.5). Columns line up well.
Line is clear and numbers align perfectly in currency columns; read as 2104.8.3.
Label and totals are clear. Arithmetic check: 700.11.7 + 270.7.11½ + 2104.8.3 = 3075.7.9½; pence fraction clearly 1/2.
This repeats the earlier 'Summa Receptorum' figure; label and number alignment clear and identical to row 7.
Label and numeric values match earlier 'Summa Solutorum' (row 12); clear alignment and legible.
Final balance is clearly written as 1326.17.6. Arithmetic check consistent with rows 13 and 14 (4402.5.3½ - 3075.7.9½ = 1326.17.6).
Section heading above signatures; text is legible and contains no monetary amounts.
First signature/title line is fairly legible: 'Johannem Collier Jones' and the title 'Rectorem' readable. No amounts present.

Second signature line is very cursive and partly faded; I can make out an initial 'J.' and an ending that resembles 'son' followed by an office abbreviation like 'Subrectorem'. Lettering is ambiguous, so transcription is uncertain.
Third signature line likely names a decanus (dean) but the name is heavily stylized and partially obscured; letters interpreted with low confidence.
Handwriting here is very condensed and ornate; appears to include 'Tho' (Thomas) and 'Burs: Sen:' (bursar senior), but the middle surname letters are largely illegible—transcription is speculative.
Lower signature looks like 'Heyes' with a bursar junior abbreviation. Several letters ambiguous; moderate-to-low confidence in exact name spelling.
Final signature in the group reads clearly as 'Williams' followed by academic initials 'A.M.'; ink and letters are legible.
Date at bottom-left is legible as 'Dec: 1mo 1819.' with clear numerals and month abbreviation; no monetary values present.
